_comment: Fleet definition for `iot deploy`. Copy to fleet.json (gitignored) and edit. Single source of truth for the deploy + the HAProxy backend list. ssh values: 'user@host' (SSH+rsync), 'interop' (a Windows node managed via WSL->Windows interop when you deploy from that box), or '' (skip; set up manually).
_models_note: 'model' is the cluster default. A node may add an optional 'models' list; nodes without one serve the default. `iot model set <name> [--node X]` pulls + records these, and HAProxy routes each request to a node that actually has the requested model.
_power_note: Optional per-node 'power' (higher = more capable; default 0). HAProxy `balance first` prefers the highest-power node for each model and only overflows to weaker ones when it's at capacity (maxconn) or down. Order is by power, not topology.
